diagrams.title: Agile Methodology Selection Decision Tree
diagrams.description: A visual flowchart guiding methodology selection based on team size, project criticality, and work predictability
elements: Start: Determine team size → <8 consider Crystal Clear, 8-20 Crystal Yellow, 20-50 Crystal Orange, >50 Crystal Red/DSDM/Scrum at Scale
Assess criticality: High criticality moves toward stricter variants, low criticality allows lighter processes
Work type assessment: Predictable/feature development → Scrum/FDD/DSDM, Unpredictable/support → Kanban/Scrumban
Output: Recommended methodology with confidence level
diagrams.purpose: Provides systematic framework for selecting appropriate agile methodology based on quantifiable project parameters, reducing arbitrary selection decisions

diagrams.title: Kanban Flow Metrics Visualization
diagrams.description: Cumulative Flow Diagram showing how to identify bottlenecks and measure flow health over time
elements: X-axis: Time (days/weeks), Y-axis: Cumulative work items
Multiple colored bands representing workflow states: To Do, In Progress, Testing, Done
Parallel bands indicate healthy flow; widening gaps reveal bottlenecks; declining 'Done' line shows throughput issues
Lead time = horizontal distance from request to completion; Cycle time = 'In Progress' to 'Done' band width
diagrams.purpose: Enables teams to visually diagnose flow problems, predict delivery times using Little's Law, and make data-driven WIP limit adjustments

diagrams.title: Lean Waste Identification Framework
diagrams.description: Seven categories of waste in software development with examples and Lean principle countermeasures
elements: 1) Partially Done Work → Amplify Learning (continuous integration)
2) Extra Features → Decide as Late as Possible (requirements stabilization)
3) Relearning → Empower Team (authority to make decisions)
4) Waiting → Deliver as Fast as Possible (reduce batch sizes)
6) Defects → Build Integrity In (quality at source)
7) Extra Process → Eliminate Waste (minimal viable processes)
diagrams.purpose: Systematically identifies non-value-adding activities, enabling targeted process improvement aligned with Lean principles
